stem: Triangle PQR is similar to triangle XYZ, where P corresponds to X and R corresponds to Z. Angles R and Z are right angles. If tan(P) = √3 and XZ = 90, what is the length of segment XY?
rationale: Since triangles PQR and XYZ are similar with right angles at R and Z, tan(X) = tan(P) = √3, meaning the angle at X is 60°. In right triangle XYZ with right angle at Z, tan(X) = YZ/XZ = √3, so YZ = 90√3. The hypotenuse XY = √(XZ² + YZ²) = √(8100 + 24300) = √32400 = 180. Choice A is XZ · (√3/3); choice C is XZ · (√3/2); choice D is the long leg YZ, not the hypotenuse.
